one_time_description: Insurance payment of more than $3.4 million received in February 2021 for Arena fire loss (occurred February 2019); not recorded in 2020 financial statements
economic_notes: Widjikiwe Gas Bar LP formed June 1, 2020 (subsequent event); gas bar operations began December 2020. Widjikiwe Holdings Corp. (WHC) controls Widjikiwe L.P. and TFN Construction L.P. WHC is in default with SOCCA loan covenants but obtained waiver. Gas Bar construction project underway on reserve territory.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). Nation is located in Quebec despite Ontario border proximity. Consolidated entity includes Widjikiwe Holdings Corp. (WHC), Widjikiwe L.P. and TFN Construction L.P. Segment totals (Note 18) differ from statement of operations due to pre-elimination presentation. Revenue total on income statement is $18,388,244 but segment disclosure shows $19,010,557 before consolidation eliminations. ISC funding calculated as sum of all ISC line items: Fixed ($1,768,311) + Block ($7,134,351) + Flexible ($322,960) + Review letter ($54,003) + Set ($2,000) + Recoverable (-$2,000) + Health Block ($1,622,935) + Health Flexible ($1,533,100) + Health Fixed ($592,254) + Health Set ($506,052) + Health Review (-$14,035) = $13,519,931 before netting; total government transfers from segment = federal ($14,035,461) + provincial ($2,124,646) = $16,160,107. Prior year comparative period noted as March 31, 2019 audited by a different auditor. Arena fire insurance of $3.4M+ received February 2021 is a subsequent event not in 2020 statements. WHC in default on SOCCA covenants but waiver obtained. No remuneration schedule found in extracted text. Council honoraries expense was $48,150.
